question: Design a `Course` class and a `Student` class. `Course` should have a `name` and a private pointer to a `Student` object (`Student* assignedStudent`) to demonstrate aggregation (a student can exist independently of a course). Implement methods to assign and unassign a student to the course.

test_cases.expected:
--- Initial State ---
Course: Calculus I, Assigned Student: No student assigned

--- Assigning Alice ---
Student 'Alice Smith' assigned to course 'Calculus I'.
Course: Calculus I, Assigned Student: Alice Smith

--- Re-assigning Alice (same student) ---
Student 'Alice Smith' is already assigned to course 'Calculus I'. No change needed.
Course: Calculus I, Assigned Student: Alice Smith

--- Assigning Bob (replaces Alice) ---
Student 'Alice Smith' unassigned from course 'Calculus I' before assigning new student.
Student 'Bob Johnson' assigned to course 'Calculus I'.
Course: Calculus I, Assigned Student: Bob Johnson

--- Unassigning student ---
Student 'Bob Johnson' unassigned from course 'Calculus I'.
Course: Calculus I, Assigned Student: No student assigned

--- Unassigning again (no student assigned) ---
No student was assigned to course 'Calculus I' to unassign. No action taken.
Course: Calculus I, Assigned Student: No student assigned

--- Attempting to assign nullptr ---
Attempted to assign a null student to course 'Calculus I'. No action taken.
Course: Calculus I, Assigned Student: No student assigned

--- Assigning Alice again for final check ---
Student 'Alice Smith' assigned to course 'Calculus I'.
Course: Calculus I, Assigned Student: Alice Smith
